vulnerability.reference: Sensitive but legitimate topics (health, legal) are misclassified due to decontextualized harmfulness scoring.
vulnerability.generated: End-users cannot override or correct inaccurate safety decisions, even when reasoning is visibly flawed, leaving them helpless.

impact.reference: Withholding of critical information, user harm, and erosion of trust in chatbots.
impact.generated: Legitimate prompts are blocked, causing censorship, user frustration, and erosion of trust in LLM chatbots.

dose_response.reference: Interpretable chain-of-thought reasoning yields overconfident safety classifications, which decontextualize sensitive topics and overblock prompts, withholding critical information and eroding trust.
dose_response.generated: Interpretable chain-of-thought harm-benefit reasoning yields overconfident but inaccurate safety classifications, through end-users interacting with LLM chatbots, triggers their inability to override visibly flawed decisions, leading to blocked legitimate prompts, censorship, frustration, and eroded trust.
